title: CARLA: Adjusted common average referencing for cortico-cortical evoked potential data
description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings from four patients undergoing single-pulse electrical stimulation, collected to investigate adjusted common average referencing methods for cortico-cortical evoked potential analysis. The data support the development and validation of CARLA, a referencing technique designed to improve signal quality in invasive electrophysiological recordings. Recordings were acquired at Mayo Clinic as part of an NIH Brain Initiative project examining processing speed across the human connectome.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG data were collected from four patients at rest during single-pulse electrical stimulation delivered at approximately 0.2 Hz frequency. Stimulation parameters: 200 microsecond duration, biphasic waveform, 6 mA amplitude. Data are formatted according to BIDS version 1.14.0.
